title: GitHub CLI
description: GitHub's official CLI for managing repos, PRs, issues, releases, and more from the terminal.

entries.label: Clone a repository
entries.description: Clones a GitHub repository. Works with your own repos or public ones.

entries.label: Create a pull request
entries.description: Creates a PR from the current branch. Use --draft for draft, --web to open in browser.

entries.label: Checkout a PR locally
entries.description: Checks out the PR branch locally. Fetches and switches in one command.

entries.label: List open issues
entries.description: Lists issues with optional filters for labels, assignees, and state.

entries.label: Create a release
entries.description: Creates a new release with tag, title, and notes. Attach files with --assets.

entries.label: Create a gist
entries.description: Creates a GitHub gist from a file. Use --public for public, default is secret.

entries.label: Make an API call
entries.description: Calls any GitHub REST/GraphQL endpoint. Use --jq for jq-style filtering.

entries.label: Authenticate
entries.description: Interactive login to GitHub. Supports token, browser, and device code auth.

entries.label: View repo info
entries.description: Shows repo description, stats, and README. Use --web to open in browser.

entries.label: Watch workflow runs
entries.description: Watches the latest GitHub Actions run in real-time with live step output.
